documents.text:
Emmanuel Oladokun
♂phone(+44)7786256943|/envel⌢peemmanueloladokun@hotmail.co.uk|/linkedinlinkedin.com/in/emmanuel-oladokun1
Summary
DPhil student with a background in generative modelling, optimisation, and statistical modelling for high-dimensional data.
Experienced in developing and analysing models on large, noisy datasets, with peer-reviewed publications and strong
mathematical foundations in probability, Bayesian inference, and statistical modelling.
Education
DPhil Engineering Science CDT, University of Oxford(viva pending; period includes internship) 2020 – 2026
Since Oct 2021, focused on formulating and analysing generative modelling approaches forhigh-dimensional datato create
synthetic datasets for downstream model training and evaluation. Usingmachine learningtechniques, designed, trained, and
evaluated models end-to-end, resulting infirst-authored publicationsandinternational conference presentations. The
developed methods generate synthetic images that demonstrably improve performance on tasks like multiclass segmentation when
augmenting real-world datasets. The most recent project usedflow matchingfor few- andone-step latent video generation.
MEng Engineering Science (2:1), University of Oxford2016 – 2020
Modules include:Machine Learning(Generative and Discriminative Models), Optimisation, Advanced Probability, Deep Learning.
A-Levels, Loreto Sixth Form: 2014 – 2016
Mathematics(A*), Further Mathematics(A), Physics(A), and Chemistry(A)
Work and Research Experience
Alan Turing Intern, GCHQ Jan 2024 – Jul 2024
–Gathered and curated datafrom multiple sources to generate a large, high-quality image dataset.
–Researched, implemented, trained, and applied state-of-the-artgenerative modelsto tackle real-world large-scale problems,
with careful attention toevaluation, robustness, scalability,andresponsible use of sensitive data.
Developer & Supervisor, University of Oxford / GE HealthCareOct 2020 – Jun 2022
–Co-developed and maintained an open-source tool for automated validation of DICOM images, ensuring data quality and
adherence to privacy standards.github.com/Extensible-Clinical-Imaging-QC-Tool/ECIQC
–Led technical design and implementation using both C++ and Python, including setting up CI/CD pipelines and automated
tests to ensure reliability as the codebase evolved.
–Supervised early-stage PhD students, mentoring on software development practices and contributing to key project deliverables.
Master’s Thesis Research, University of OxfordOct 2019 – Jun 2020
–Developedstatistical time-series modelsto detect and characterise circadian dynamics inirregularly sampled data.
–Performed large-scale parameter estimation and probabilistic inference on4M noisy recordsfrom208,948 admissions,
including hypothesis testing and model comparison across sub-populations.
–Implemented non-linear least squaresoptimisation(Levenberg–Marquardt) and compared alternative basis functions to
improve model fit under missing and noisy observations.
–Awarded71 (First Class)for the thesis.
Software Engineering Intern, SchlumbergerJul 2019 – Sep 2019
–Reduced computational costs by 30–50%within a production reservoir simulator by usingmachine learningmodels.
–Successfully developedmachine learningmodels usingScikit-Learnand TensorFlow.
–Swiftly adapted to a newhigh-pressure environment. Findings of this internship have led to a publication.
Undergraduate Research Assistant, University of OxfordJul 2018 – Sep 2018
–Developed a dynamic mass–spring model of a wind turbine subject to wind loads, formulating and solving the governing
differential equationsin MATLAB.
–Implemented numerical integration schemes including Euler andRunge–Kutta methodsto simulate structural response
under extreme loading conditions.
–Validated simulations through experimental testing in theUK’s largest functioning wave tank.

Achievements
Publications (top-tier applied ML/CV venues)
–Oladokun, E. et al. (2025); From Transthoracic to Transesophageal: Cross-Modality Generation using LoRA Diffusion;
International Workshop on Advances in Simplifying Medical Ultrasound (ASMUS), held at the International Conference on
Medical Image Computing and Computer-Assisted Intervention (MICCAI).
Best Paper Award;Diffusion Models;Cross-Modality Learning;Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning
–Oladokun, E. et al. (2023); Transesophageal Echocardiography Generation using Anatomical Models; International Conference
on Medical Image Computing and Computer-Assisted Intervention (MICCAI).
Generative Adversarial Networks;Contrastive Unpaired Translation;Synthetic Data
–Oladokun, E et al. (2020); Machine-Learning Informed Prediction of Linear Solver Tolerance for Non-Linear Solution Methods
in Numerical Simulation; ECMOR XVII
Random Forest Regression; Linear Algebra
Awards
–Best Paper (Sep 2025):Awarded’Best Paper’at ASMUS-MICCAI 2025 for paper addressing diffusion-based image
generation using data from multiple domains.
– Google Hackathon (Oct 2019):Co-conceived and developed amachine learning–based application to support handwriting
learning for people with cerebral palsy and related needs. As a team, refined the concept and delivered a workingprototype in
less than 24 hours, earning 2nd place out of 12 teams.github.com/HandRiGht/handright/blob/master/README.md
– Physics Olympiad (Apr 2016):Awarded a Commendation
– UKMT Maths Challenge (Nov 2015):Awarded a Silver Certificate.
Skills
Programming Languages & Libraries: Python, MATLAB, C++;NumPy, SciPy, Scikit-Learn, HuggingFace,
PyTorch
Methods:Generative modelling, (latent) diffusion models, foundation models, flow matching,Bayesian inference
(MLE, MAP), Markov chains, representation learning, contrastive learning, transformer architectures
NVIDIA Certification:Rapid Application Development with Large Language Models (LLMs)
Leadership and Service
Engineering Tutor, St Peter’s College 2022
–Delivered tutorials in Mathematics and Physicsfor first-year engineering students, and assessed problem sheets
Volunteer, THRIVE Nov 2018 – Dec 2022
–Equipped future leaders in deprived communities with key skills and values by leading a weekly sports session.
–Ensuring children are safe and well provided for during excursions, contributing to a positive environment and lasting memories.
Student Ambassador, SchlumbergerSep 2019 – Sep 2020
–Actively publicised opportunities at Schlumberger and increased awareness across the university.
–Shared internship experience at career fairs and provided consultations for potential applicants.
Alumni Relations Officer, St Peter’s College, University of OxfordNov 2016 – Jun 2018
–Established strong ties as an ambassador through connecting undergraduates with alumni, and networking with professionals.
–Raised£5,862for the college during a telethon and learned protocols for dealing with sensitive and confidential information.
Interests
Football:Played collegiate football including inter-college ‘Cuppers’ competitions at Oxford; currently play for fun.
Drumming:Active drummer, rehearsing independently, corporately, and performing monthly in live services to
congregations of up to 200 people.

Dear Hiring Team,
I am writing to apply for the Research Scientist Intern position at 
Spotify, based in London. I am currently a DPhil student in 
Engineering Science at the University of Oxford, awaiting my viva, 
with a research background in applied machine learning, computer 
vision, and generative modelling for image and video data.
My doctoral research has focused on developing, training, and 
rigorously evaluating generative and representation learning models 
on large, noisy real-world datasets. I have worked extensively with 
diffusion-based models, flow matching, contrastive learning, and 
foundation models, including fine-tuning and adapting pre-trained 
models for downstream tasks. This work has resulted in first-
authored publications at leading applied machine learning and 
computer vision venues, including MICCAI, and has required careful 
attention to evaluation, robustness, and responsible use of 
sensitive data.
Alongside my academic work, I have gained applied research and 
engineering experience through industry internships, including at 
GCHQ and Schlumberger. In these roles, I worked on real-world 
machine learning problems involving large-scale data, end-to-end 
model development, and performance optimisation, including reducing 
computational costs in production systems. These experiences have 
strengthened my interest in application-oriented research that 
connects strong methodological foundations with practical impact.
I am particularly excited by this role at Spotify because of its 
focus on understanding and improving how users interact with 
audiovisual content at scale. The opportunity to apply machine 
learning and representation learning techniques to problems such as 
discovery, moderation, and exploration of audio and video content, 
while contributing to a broader research community through 
publication, strongly aligns with my interests and experience.
I would welcome the opportunity to contribute my research background 
and applied machine learning experience to Spotify’s research teams 
during the summer internship programme. Thank you for considering my 
application.
Yours sincerely,
Emmanuel Oladokun

Dear Hiring Team,

I am writing to apply for the Research Scientist Intern position at Spotify, based in London. I am currently a DPhil student in Engineering Science at the University of Oxford, awaiting my viva, with a research background in applied machine learning, computer vision, and generative modelling for image and video data.

My doctoral research has focused on developing, training, and rigorously evaluating generative and representation learning models on large, noisy real-world datasets. I have worked extensively with diffusion-based models, flow matching, contrastive learning, and foundation models, including fine-tuning and adapting pre-trained models for downstream tasks. This work has resulted in first-authored publications at leading applied machine learning and computer vision venues, including MICCAI, and has required careful attention to evaluation, robustness, and responsible use of sensitive data.

Alongside my academic work, I have gained applied research and engineering experience through industry internships, including at GCHQ and Schlumberger. In these roles, I worked on real-world machine learning problems involving large-scale data, end-to-end model development, and performance optimisation, including reducing computational costs in production systems. These experiences have strengthened my interest in application-oriented research that connects strong methodological foundations with practical impact.

I am particularly excited by this role at Spotify because of its focus on understanding and improving how users interact with audiovisual content at scale. The opportunity to apply machine learning and representation learning techniques to problems such as discovery, moderation, and exploration of audio and video content, while contributing to a broader research community through publication, strongly aligns with my interests and experience.

I would welcome the opportunity to contribute my research background and applied machine learning experience to Spotify’s research teams during the summer internship programme. Thank you for considering my application.

Yours sincerely,
Emmanuel Oladokun

Dear Hiring Team,

I am writing to apply for the Machine Learning Engineer position within Waymo’s AI Foundations team. I am a DPhil student in Engineering Science at the University of Oxford, currently awaiting my viva, with a background in applied machine learning, computer vision, and generative modelling, and experience working with large-scale, real-world data.

My doctoral research has focused on designing, training, and rigorously evaluating deep generative and representation learning models for image and video data. This includes work with diffusion-based models, flow matching, contrastive learning, and parameter-efficient fine-tuning of pre-trained foundation models. A central aspect of my research has been careful evaluation under noise, data scarcity, and distribution shift, with an emphasis on robustness and reliability in safety-critical settings. This work has resulted in first-authored publications at leading applied machine learning and computer vision venues.

Alongside academic research, I have gained applied engineering experience through industry internships, including at GCHQ and Schlumberger. In these roles, I worked on end-to-end machine learning systems involving data curation, model training, evaluation, and optimisation, including reducing computational costs in production simulators. These experiences strengthened my interest in framing open-ended real-world problems as well-defined learning tasks and scaling solutions to operate under practical constraints.

While my background is not in reinforcement learning or imitation learning directly, much of my work sits at the foundation of these approaches, particularly in representation learning, generative modelling, and robust evaluation. I am keen to apply and extend this experience to learning from demonstration, simulation-based training, and safety-critical decision making in autonomous systems, and I am motivated by Waymo’s emphasis on rigorous validation and real-world impact.

I am particularly excited by the opportunity to work within Waymo’s AI Foundations team, collaborating with researchers and engineers across Alphabet to develop scalable machine learning solutions that contribute directly to safe autonomous driving. I would welcome the opportunity to contribute my experience while learning from experts working at the forefront of large-scale, safety-critical machine learning.

Thank you for considering my application.

Yours sincerely,
Emmanuel Oladokun

Dear Hiring Team,

I am writing to apply for the Machine Learning Engineer position within Epic Games. I am an applied machine learning researcher with a DPhil in Engineering Science from the University of Oxford, currently awaiting my viva, with a background in generative modelling and computer vision, and experience applying and fine-tuning pre-trained foundation models in collaboration with industry partners.

My doctoral research has focused on designing, training, and rigorously evaluating deep generative models for image and video data, including diffusion models, flow-based methods, and parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques. I have led projects end to end, from problem formulation and data collection through model development, evaluation, and publication at major international conferences. A central part of my work has been ensuring that trained models are reliable, interpretable, and robust enough for real-world use, particularly in data-constrained and safety-critical settings.

Alongside academic research, I have gained applied engineering experience through internships in high-pressure environments, including at GCHQ and Schlumberger. In these roles, I worked on applied machine learning systems, including optimising models to significantly reduce computational cost and collaborating closely with engineers and domain specialists. These experiences have shaped my interest in building machine learning tools that are not only technically strong but also scalable, maintainable, and useful to others working with them.

I am particularly drawn to this role at Epic because of its focus on integrating machine learning directly into tools that empower creators. The opportunity to work at the intersection of vision, language, and applied machine learning, and to contribute to systems that support content creation, automation, and development workflows within the Unreal ecosystem, strongly aligns with how I approach applied research and engineering.

I would welcome the opportunity to contribute my experience in generative modelling, rigorous model evaluation, and applied machine learning engineering to Epic’s teams, while learning from experts working on state-of-the-art engine and developer technologies. Thank you for considering my application.

Yours sincerely,
Emmanuel Oladokun
